status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.name: Snowflake objects built via CoCo
features.description: Run the prompts in snowflake/coco-prompts.md against a real Snowflake account: CLIMATE_AG_COPILOT database/OPS schema, the FARMS/WEATHER_READINGS/SENSOR_READINGS/RISK_ASSESSMENTS/WORK_ORDERS/CROP_HISTORY tables, and seed data (including historical yield seasons). This is a prerequisite for every backend feature below.
features.user_visible_behavior: Querying the tables in Snowsight returns the expected schema and seed rows.
verification: Every prompt in snowflake/coco-prompts.md has its 'Result' line filled in
SELECT * from each table in CLIMATE_AG_COPILOT.OPS returns rows without error
evidence: 2026-07-13: All 5 prompts in snowflake/coco-prompts.md have 'Result' lines filled in (db/tables, seed data, semantic view, Cortex Agent, verification queries).

2026-07-13: backend/.env populated with real SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT/USER/ROLE/WAREHOUSE/DATABASE/SCHEMA/PAT. Created backend/venv, installed requirements.txt, and ran a live query via app.services.snowflake_client.run_query() against each table: FARMS=15, WEATHER_READINGS=450, SENSOR_READINGS=450, RISK_ASSESSMENTS=72, CROP_HISTORY=45 rows (all match coco-prompts.md counts). WORK_ORDERS=0 rows, which is expected — that table is seeded by app logic in feat-004, not by the CoCo seed-data prompt.
features.notes: Verified against the real Snowflake account this session. WORK_ORDERS is empty by design until feat-004 writes to it.

features.name: Wire weather ingestion + Snowflake write into /workflow/run
features.description: Replace the stub in backend/app/main.py:run_daily_workflow (steps 1-2) with real calls: fetch per-farm forecasts via app/services/weather_client.py, then write WEATHER_READINGS rows via app/services/snowflake_client.py.
features.user_visible_behavior: POST /workflow/run writes real weather rows to WEATHER_READINGS for every farm in FARMS.
verification: uvicorn app.main:app --reload, then curl -X POST localhost:8000/workflow/run
New rows appear in WEATHER_READINGS with today's timestamp
evidence: 2026-07-13: Added weather_client.get_today_reading() (reduces Open-Meteo forecast to today's rainfall/temp/humidity) and snowflake_client.execute_many() (INSERT via executemany). Wired both into run_daily_workflow steps 1-2: query FARMS, fetch a live forecast per farm, bulk-insert into WEATHER_READINGS.

2026-07-13: Ran uvicorn app.main:app against the real backend/venv + live Snowflake account. WEATHER_READINGS row count was 450 before POST /workflow/run, 465 after (+15, one per seeded farm). curl response: {"farms_assessed":15,...}. Sampled the 5 newest rows via a direct query — real Open-Meteo values (e.g. FARM_ID 15: rainfall_mm=16.2, temp_c=26.65, humidity_pct=92.08) with today's timestamp and source='open-meteo'.
features.notes: Steps 3-5 of run_daily_workflow (risk assessment, work orders, real briefing summary) remain stubbed — that's feat-003/004/006.

features.name: Cortex Agent risk assessment call
features.description: backend/app/services/cortex_agent_client.py is a placeholder pending the exact Cortex Agents REST API request/response shape (see its module docstring). Confirm the real endpoint/payload against Snowflake's docs and the FARM_OPS_AGENT built in feat-001, then wire step 3 of run_daily_workflow to call it and parse risk + recommendations per farm.
features.user_visible_behavior: POST /workflow/run returns real flood/drought/disease risk levels per farm instead of an empty list.
verification: ask_agent() returns a real response from FARM_OPS_AGENT for a sample prompt
run_daily_workflow populates high_risk_farms from the agent's assessment, not a hardcoded value

features.name: Plot/risk read endpoints + work order approval endpoints
features.description: Step 4 of run_daily_workflow: for farms flagged high-risk by feat-003, create WORK_ORDERS rows with status pending_approval. Add the read/write endpoints the frontend contract in docs/ui-build-plan.md needs: GET /plots (plot id, name, location, latest risk level), GET /plots/{id}/risk (risk narrative + generated work order), POST /workorders/{id}/approve and POST /workorders/{id}/reject (update WORK_ORDERS.status, approved_by, approved_at).
features.user_visible_behavior: GET /plots lists plots with risk badges; GET /plots/{id}/risk returns the Cortex Agent's narrative + recommended work order; calling approve/reject flips a work order's status and it disappears from the pending list.
verification: curl -X POST /workflow/run creates WORK_ORDERS rows for seeded high-risk farms
curl GET /plots and /plots/{id}/risk return real Snowflake-backed data
Approve/reject endpoint updates the row and is reflected in a subsequent GET

features.name: Three-screen frontend (plot list, risk + work order, approval history/briefing)
features.description: Scaffold frontend/ per frontend/README.md (create-next-app). Build the 3 screens defined in docs/ui-build-plan.md, in that doc's build order: Screen 2 (risk assessment + work order panel, approve/reject) first against feat-004's /plots/{id}/risk and approve/reject endpoints, then Screen 1 (plot list) against /plots, then Screen 3 (approval history + daily briefing) against feat-006's /briefing/today.
features.user_visible_behavior: Opening localhost:3000 shows the plot list; clicking a plot shows the Cortex Agent's risk narrative and recommended work order with approve/reject; a third screen shows today's approved/rejected work orders plus a generated daily briefing.
verification: npm run build succeeds in frontend/
Manual walkthrough: load /, open a plot, approve its work order, confirm it appears in the approval history screen
features.notes: frontend/ has no package.json yet — this feature includes the initial scaffold, not just the pages.

features.name: Daily briefing summary assembly + /briefing/today endpoint
features.description: Step 5 of run_daily_workflow: replace the hardcoded DailyBriefing fields (date, farms_assessed, summary) with real values derived from feat-002/003/004 results. Add GET /briefing/today, which aggregates today's approved/rejected work orders and returns the generated summary, per the contract in docs/ui-build-plan.md.
features.user_visible_behavior: The DailyBriefing returned by /workflow/run accurately reflects the day's real farms_assessed count, high_risk_farms, and work_orders_created; GET /briefing/today returns the same day's approved/rejected list plus a natural-language summary.
verification: curl -X POST /workflow/run and confirm every field matches what was actually written to Snowflake this run
curl GET /briefing/today after approving at least one work order and confirm it reflects that approval
